entries.title: Browse every bus, work order, and part in one searchable list
entries.body: New Buses, Work Orders, and Parts pages in the sidebar let you search, sort, and filter across the full fleet. Each row opens the same detail panel you already use from Fleet Overview and the Service Board.

entries.title: Click a part to see stock by garage and which jobs are using it
entries.body: Tapping any part — from Parts at Risk, a work order, or the new catalog — opens a panel showing stock per garage, the active work orders consuming it, and a "running low" callout before you stock out.

entries.title: See blocked work at a glance with the Repairs on Hold KPI
entries.body: The Running card has been replaced with Repairs on Hold — click it to drill into every held job sorted by dwell time, with block reason, ETA, and a tip when the part is in stock at the other garage.

entries.title: Request a cross-garage parts transfer from the work order
entries.body: When a part is out locally but stocked at the other garage, mechanics can now hit "Request Transfer" right from the work order panel. A toast fires and the button flips to "Transfer requested" so ops know help is on the way.

entries.title: Accessibility repairs auto-escalate to Critical
entries.body: Work orders touching wheelchair lifts, ramps, securement, or kneeling valves now automatically flag as Critical with an ADA badge on cards and tracker rows, so repairs that keep buses accessible to riders never get lost in the queue.

entries.title: New repairs start in Intake and move cleanly through to Done
entries.body: New repair orders and scheduled PMs now enter at Intake (not Triage), you pick the garage when you log them, the Triage button actually advances the card, and marking a job Done updates service history, resets PM mileage, and decrements parts stock.

entries.title: Schedule a PM on any bus, not just overdue ones
entries.body: The Schedule PM service button is now available for every bus in the fleet, so ops can plan preventive work ahead of the interval instead of waiting for it to tip overdue.

entries.title: Advance a work order without leaving the detail panel
entries.body: Mechanics get a "Move to [Next Stage]" button inside the WO detail panel. No more closing the drawer just to drag a card on the kanban. The same parts-gating and toasts apply.

entries.title: Tap a queue-by-stage pill to filter the board
entries.body: The stage pills above the work order list are now real filters — tap to focus on one stage, tap the × to clear, or hit "Clear all" to reset stage and severity together.

entries.title: See how long a bus sat in each pipeline stage
entries.body: The work order tracker now shows dwell time per stage, and "Total shop time" replaces "In current stage" — so you can see where a job actually got stuck, not just where it is now.

entries.title: Spot which repairs are dragging past the 12-hour median
entries.body: The In Maintenance KPI calls out how many active jobs are above the 12-hour median (coral when any slip), the drilldown splits "Above/Below average," and the In the Shop list finally sorts by total dwell — so a two-day repair doesn't sink below one that arrived this morning.

entries.title: Availability and PM bars now show how far you've slipped
entries.body: The Availability card uses three color tiers (coral under 84%, amber 84–95%, green at 95% target) and annotates "X pts below 95% target." The PM progress bar splits into amber for the 6k-mi interval and red extending for how far past it you are — a 57-mile slip is a thin red sliver, 1,700 miles is a wide red zone.

entries.title: A cleaner, calmer FleetPulse
entries.body: A top-to-bottom design pass: tighter typography and density, Nucleo icons in place of emoji, neutralized pipeline colors, aligned badges and pills, a new burnt-orange bus favicon, and "V2" labels where "Coming Soon" used to sit.

entries.title: Mobile and touch polish across cards, sheets, and the kanban
entries.body: Fixed mobile card height, close button, and scroll; swapped the drag-bar for an X on bottom sheets; added a hold-to-drag delay with drop indicators; removed touch drag from the kanban so horizontal scroll actually works; added back/forward nav buttons for touch devices.

entries.title: PM compliance rate and MTTR now on the dashboard
entries.body: Ops now sees Preventive Maintenance compliance rate and mean time to repair alongside fleet availability — the two numbers fleet managers track most.

entries.title: KPI cards show whether things are trending up or down
entries.body: Every KPI on the Ops dashboard now shows a directional delta so you can tell at a glance whether availability, repair time, and compliance are improving or slipping.

entries.title: "Schedule PM service" stays in reach as you scroll
entries.body: The button to schedule a preventive maintenance no longer disappears when you scroll through an overdue bus's detail panel — it sticks to the bottom of the drawer.

entries.title: Fixed: search, stale panels, and depot predictions
entries.body: Squashed four bugs: bus ID search not matching, forms remembering stale values, side panels not refreshing on new selections, and depot predictions falling out of sync.

entries.title: See parts risk before a job stalls
entries.body: Ops gets a stockout risk flag on any part that might run dry before a job completes. Mechanics see a line-by-line parts list directly on each work order card.

entries.title: Scheduling a PM is now an Ops call
entries.body: "Schedule PM service" moved from the mechanic board to the Ops view, where the people who decide when to pull a bus out of service can act on it directly.

entries.title: Tap any service history entry to see the original work order
entries.body: Clicking a past repair in a bus's service history now opens the full historical work order — what was done, who did it, and what parts were used.

entries.title: Mobile polish: depot switcher, bottom sheets, and layout fixes
entries.body: Fixed the mobile depot switcher overlapping content, bottom-sheet dismiss breaking after a scroll, Service Board layout clipping on small screens, and the sidebar staying open after tapping a nav link.

entries.title: Click any work order to drill into a full detail panel
entries.body: Tapping a work order anywhere in the app now opens a side panel with the full job — severity, stage, assigned mechanic, parts, and notes — without leaving your current view.

entries.title: Click any KPI to see the buses behind the number
entries.body: Every Ops KPI card is now drillable — clicking the count opens the list of buses that make up that figure so you can act on the data, not just read it.

entries.title: Cross-garage visibility and aging tags
entries.body: Bus detail panels now show repairs from both garages so teams stop duplicating work. Work orders that have been sitting in a stage too long grow an aging tag so nothing falls through the cracks.

entries.title: Pick your depot once — both views remember it
entries.body: Selecting North or South garage in the depot switcher now applies to Fleet Overview and Service Board simultaneously, so you stop re-filtering when you switch views.

entries.title: New sidebar with cleaner navigation
entries.body: The nav rail is now a Linear-style sidebar with labelled icons for Fleet Overview, Service Board, and settings — easier to read and a faster way to switch between your two main views.

entries.title: See what just shipped from your profile menu
entries.body: Click your avatar in the bottom-left of the nav rail to open a profile menu with a What's New entry that lists every recent change to FleetPulse.

entries.title: Assign a mechanic when you log a new repair
entries.body: The intake form now lets you pick the mechanic who'll own the work order, and the layout fits a phone so you can do it from the bay.

entries.title: Log new repairs and advance work orders in one tap
entries.body: Mechanics can now log a fresh work order from intake and push cards through the swim lanes without leaving the board.

entries.title: Focus the mechanic board on your work, drag cards to advance them
entries.body: Filter the swim lanes to just the work orders assigned to you, and drag a card from one stage to the next instead of opening a menu.

entries.title: Watch fleet availability trend toward your 95% target
entries.body: The Ops dashboard now shows a 30-day availability sparkline against the 95% service target so you can spot drift before it bites.

entries.title: Move work forward as a mechanic, plan tomorrow as ops
entries.body: Mechanic cards now show parts status and one-click stage advance. Ops gets a stage-bottleneck bar and a severity filter to decide where to send help.
